444.title: Édesapám, kisangyalom
444.summary: Tíz éve halt meg Esterházy Péter, aki úgy írt, ahogy más levegőt vesz. Urfi Péter könyvében van egy fejezet róla, ezt közöljük az évfordulón.

444.title: Mi a különbség Isten és Esterházy Péter között?
444.summary: A különbség jól látható, Isten mindenhol ott van, ezzel szemben Esterházy Péter is mindenhol ott van, csak itt nincs. Tíz éve ment el a magyar irodalom lifegő bokájú Michael Jacksonja.

444.summary: A miniszterelnök megköszönte a szigetek munkáját.

444.summary: A kuvaiti-magyar rapper a kampányban jelent meg Orbán eseményein, vagánynak tartotta, hogy találkozhat a miniszterelnökkel, bírja a magyarokat.

BBC.title: Police say Ann Widdecombe killed in 'targeted attack' as motive investigated
BBC.summary: Counter terror police say they have obtained a warrant to hold a 28-year-old suspect for up to seven days.

BBC.title: Trump scraps threat of 20% fee on Hormuz cargo as US resumes blockade of Iran ports
BBC.summary: Trump drops a 24-hour-old vow to charge cargo ships for using the Strait as the US continues its battle to break Iran's hold on the waterway.

BBC.title: Starmer and Burnham hail Hillsborough Law as MPs give approval
BBC.summary: The legislation cleared the House of Commons in Sir Keir Starmer's final days as prime minister.

BBC.title: Midnight social media curfew proposed for older UK teens
BBC.summary: Teens will be able to opt out of the restrictions - campaigners have criticised them as being piecemeal.

BBC.title: Tyrannosaurus rex most expensive dinosaur ever sold
BBC.summary: The dinosaur skeleton, which roamed the planet 67 million years ago, was sold by Sotheby's in New York.

BBC.title: Review of MP security needed after Widdecombe death, Burnham says
BBC.summary: The expected next prime minister says politics has "darkened" in the decade he has been away from Westminster.

BBC.title: Scott Mills was highest-paid BBC star before sacking
BBC.summary: The former Radio 2 breakfast host earned about £745,000 from the BBC in the last financial year.

BBC.title: Southport victim's dad accuses ambulance staff of breaching trust
BBC.summary: North West Ambulance Service is investigating whether staff inappropriately accessed medical records.

BBC.title: Starmer becomes first UK PM to receive top French honour
BBC.summary: France's President Emmanuel Macron praised Starmer's "decency" and contribution to Ukraine and the security of Europe.

BBC.title: UK wasted £10bn on PPE that left NHS staff poorly protected, Covid inquiry finds
BBC.summary: Healthcare staff were unable to properly protect themselves, or those in their care, from dangerous infections, says the latest inquiry report.

BBC.title: Billionaire Warren Buffett stops donations to Bill Gates charity
BBC.summary: The investor's decision comes weeks after Gates' appearance before US Congress over his links to Jeffrey Epstein.

BBC.title: Henderson & Quansah only England absentees for Argentina
BBC.summary: Jordan Henderson and Jarell Quansah are set to be the only players unavailable for England's World Cup semi-final against Argentina.

BBC.title: World Cup final half-time to last up to 25 minutes
BBC.summary: The half-time break in Sunday's World Cup final is expected to last between 20 and 25 minutes.

BBC.title: He walked for 47% of the World Cup - the evolution of Messi
BBC.summary: Lionel Messi is looking to beat England and reach a third World Cup final with Argentina. Guillem Balague looks at the evolution of one of the game's all-time greats.

BBC.title: Balogun expected controversy after World Cup ban waived
BBC.summary: United States striker Folarin Balogun knew the decision by Fifa to suspend his one-game ban at the World Cup was going to bring "a lot of controversy" and said it brought "nerves" on his team-mates.

TechCrunch.title: OpenAI’s first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move
TechCrunch.summary: OpenAI's first hardware device is reported to be a screenless, AI-guided smart speaker that can move. Weird enough for you?

TechCrunch.title: OpenAI pushes back on Apple trade secret lawsuit
TechCrunch.summary: OpenAI has issued another statement on the lawsuit, this time suggesting it lacks merit.

TechCrunch.title: OpenAI’s new flagship model deletes files on its own, people keep warning
TechCrunch.summary: A number of social media posts claim that GPT-5.6 Sol deleted files and data without warning. OpenAI had basically disclosed the problem in June.

TechCrunch.title: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
TechCrunch.summary: If you’ve been waiting to try Apple’s revamped Siri without installing a developer beta, you now can. The company on Tuesday released the iOS 27 public beta, giving iPhone owners early access to its AI-powered assistant and other new features before the software’s official launch this fall.

TechCrunch.title: Anthropic’s newest ad is creeping people out
TechCrunch.summary: Anthropic has consistently attempted to depict itself as the ethical foil to other AI companies. This latest marketing stunt — which leans into criticism of AI as a way to make Anthropic seem aware of the responsibility it carries — would appear to be more of the same.

TechCrunch.title: The founder of Hinge raised $18M to build a new AI dating service, Overtone
TechCrunch.summary: Overtone describes itself as "a voice- and audio-forward service, enabled by AI, that provides highly curated introductions."

TechCrunch.title: Lucid Motors denies report it’s considering bankruptcy
TechCrunch.summary: The company said the "rumors are completely false" after its stock sank more than 50% on a report that it was weighing the option.

TechCrunch.title: Google faces another AI training lawsuit from major publishers
TechCrunch.summary: Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, and other publishers allege that Google trained its AI on copyrighted works without the necessary permissions.

TechCrunch.title: DeepMind CEO calls for an independent standards body to regulate frontier AI
TechCrunch.summary: DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis is proposing an AI "standards body" modeled after FINRA, to test frontier models and develop best practices for their release.

TechCrunch.title: DeepSeek reportedly in talks to raise $1.5B, then IPO
TechCrunch.summary: DeepSeek, the Chinese large language model developer, is said to be preparing for a 2027 IPO debut as it also looks to raise around $1.5 billion in new funds at a $71 billion valuation.

TechCrunch.title: Meta’s Adam Mosseri says AI token budgets could soon be capped per engineer
TechCrunch.summary: Instagram head Adam Mosseri believes companies will eventually need to manage AI token spending the same way they manage payroll or other operating expenses, predicting that engineers could soon face limits on how much they spend using AI tools.

TechCrunch.title: Google Images gets a Pinterest-like redesign focused on discovery
TechCrunch.summary: Now, when users navigate to Google Images, they'll see a "For You" gallery of images tailored to their interests and browsing history.

TechCrunch.title: New York State halts construction of all new data centers
TechCrunch.summary: New York has become the first state to temporarily halt approval of large data centers, as Gov. Kathy Hochul argues the AI-driven building boom shouldn’t come at the expense of higher electricity costs, water supplies, or local control.

TechCrunch.title: Iran abused mobile networks’ vulnerabilities to locate US military in the Middle East, report says
TechCrunch.summary: The Iranian government exploited well-known flaws in cellphone networks to locate and then strike U.S. military personnel in the build-up and beginning of the war.

TechCrunch.title: I’m de-influencing you from buying the RingConn 3 (even though it’s pretty)
TechCrunch.summary: The RingConn 3 actually looks like real jewelry, not a wearable -- but its fitness tracking and headache detection features are disappointing.
